assessment.title: M5 Impaired Driving and Alcohol Assessment

material.mattext: The accurate chemical name for the alcohol found in alcoholic beverages is
Alcohol is created through a process known as
Which type of alcoholic beverage has the highest concentration of alcohol by volume?
Which area of the brain is the first to be affected noticeably by alcohol that is absorbed into the body?
People are considered to be legally impaired in Canada if they
drink one glass of wine in three hours
have more than 80 mg of alcohol per 100 ml of blood
have more than 50 mg of alcohol in 100 ml of blood
drink two cans of beer in two hours
The blood alcohol concentration of a person increases
more quickly if something haws been eaten
more slowly if some vigorous exercise has been completed
Which country has a higher BAC legal limit than Canada?
How does the concentration of alcohol in blood compare with the concentration of alcohol in the alveoli?
Alcohol is broken down into acetic acid in the alveoli.
The alcohol concentration is 2100 times greater in blood.
The alcohol concentration is the same in both.
Alcohol is converted into sulfuric acid by the liver.
The Breathalyzer became more popular with law enforcement agencies than the Drunkometer because it
could determine if a person had consumed any drugs
could prove what type of alcoholic beverage was consumed
was less expensive and did not need any repairs
was more portable and did not need recalibration every time it was moved
Which reactant in the test vial of the Breathalyzer is a catalyst?
When alcohol is absorbed into the blood and while it is transported through the blood, it does not
After the solution in a test vial has been exposed to alcohol, it is
compared with a solution in a control vial
The primary colour change that occurs in the solution of a test vial that has been exposed to alcohol is
The reactants found in the glass test vial before an individual breathes into the Breathalyzer are
acetic acid, sulfuric acid, silver nitrate, and water
alcohol, potassium dichromate, sulfuric acid, water, and silver nitrate
potassium dichromate, sulfuric acid, water, and silver nitrate
chromium sulfate, potassium sulfate, acetic acid, water, and silver nitrate
The type of technology the Intoxilyzer uses to analyze an alcohol breath sample is
The ideal temperature of a breath sample blown into an Intoxilyzer is
In the Intoxilyzer, when infrared light absorbed by alcohol passes through the spinning filter wheel to the photocell system, it is converted into
Unlike the Breathalyzer, the Intoxilyzer eliminates the need for
